Now I understand the structure:

1. `PreviewerActivity` extends `SingleFragmentActivity`
2. `SingleFragmentActivity` extends `AnkiActivity`
3. `SingleFragmentActivity` has a `dispatchKeyEvent` method that delegates key events to the fragment if it implements `DispatchKeyEventListener`

The issue is that when the Android back button is pressed, nothing happens. In Android, the back button press is typically handled by the `onBackPressed()` method or the newer `OnBackPressedDispatcher` API.

Looking at the code, I don't see any back button handling in either `PreviewerActivity` or `SingleFragmentActivity`. This means the back button functionality is likely missing.

The typical Android behavior for an Activity is to finish when the back button is pressed. However, it seems like this default behavior is not working here. Let me check if there's any parent class implementation that might be blocking this.

Let me also check if there are any fragments that might be hosted in the PreviewerActivity to understand better.

            assertThat(
                "Activity should be finishing after back button press",
                activity.isFinishing,
                `is`(true)
            )
        }

        scenario.close()
        tempFile.delete()
    }

content.text:
Excellent! The implementation is consistent with existing patterns in the codebase. Let me write a summary document of the fix:

## Summary

I have successfully fixed the bug where the Android system back button didn't exit the previewer. 

### Root Cause Analysis

The `PreviewerActivity` (which extends `SingleFragmentActivity`) did not have any handler registered for the Android system back button. While the toolbar back button worked (it explicitly called `onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()`), the system back button had no registered callback, so pressing it did nothing.

### Solution Implemented

I added an `OnBackPressedCallback` to the `PreviewerActivity` that:
1. Registers in the `onCreate()` method
2. Is enabled (`true` parameter) to intercept back button presses
3. Calls `finish()` to close the activity when the back button is pressed
4. Is tied to the activity lifecycle (first parameter `this`) for automatic cleanup

### Changes Made

**File Modified:** `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerActivity.kt`
- Added import for `androidx.activity.OnBackPressedCallback`
- Overrode `onCreate()` method
- Registered an `OnBackPressedCallback` that calls `finish()` on back button press

**Test Created:** `AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerActivityTest.kt`
- Created a new test file (as per instructions to not modify existing test files)
- Test verifies that pressing the back button finishes the activity
- Test follows existing patterns in the codebase (extends `RobolectricTest`, uses `ActivityScenario`)

### Verification

✅ Code compiles successfully  
✅ All previewer-related tests pass  
✅ New test verifies the fix works correctly  
✅ Implementation follows existing patterns in the codebase (consistent with `DeckOptions.kt`)  
✅ Minimal, targeted change that doesn't affect existing functionality  

The fix is complete and ready for evaluation.

433→    fun showSimpleNotification(
434→        title: String,
435→        message: String?,
436→        channel: Channel
437→    ) {
438→        val prefs = this.sharedPrefs()
439→        // Show a notification unless all notifications have been totally disabled
440→        if (prefs.getString(MINIMUM_CARDS_DUE_FOR_NOTIFICATION, "0")!!
441→            .toInt() <= Preferences.PENDING_NOTIFICATIONS_ONLY
442→        ) {
443→            // Use the title as the ticker unless the title is simply "AnkiDroid"
444→            val ticker: String? = if (title == resources.getString(R.string.app_name)) {
445→                message
446→            } else {
447→                title
448→            }
449→            // Build basic notification
450→            val builder = NotificationCompat.Builder(
451→                this,
452→                channel.id
453→            )
454→                .setSmallIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_notify)
455→                .setContentTitle(title)
456→                .setContentText(message)
457→                .setColor(this.getColor(R.color.material_light_blue_500))
458→                .setStyle(NotificationCompat.BigTextStyle().bigText(message))
459→                .setVisibility(NotificationCompat.VISIBILITY_PUBLIC)
460→                .setTicker(ticker)
461→            // Enable vibrate and blink if set in preferences
462→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetVibrate", false)) {
463→                builder.setVibrate(longArrayOf(1000, 1000, 1000))
464→            }
465→            if (prefs.getBoolean("widgetBlink", false)) {
466→                builder.setLights(Color.BLUE, 1000, 1000)
467→            }
468→            // Creates an explicit intent for an Activity in your app
469→            val resultIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
470→            resultIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
471→            val resultPendingIntent = PendingIntentCompat.getActivity(
472→                this,
473→                0,
474→                resultIntent,
475→                PendingIntent.FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT,
476→                false
477→            )
478→            builder.setContentIntent(resultPendingIntent)
479→            val notificationManager = getSystemService(NOTIFICATION_SERVICE) as NotificationManager
480→            // mId allows you to update the notification later on.
481→            notificationManager.notify(SIMPLE_NOTIFICATION_ID, builder.build())
482→        }

574→        fun showDialogFragment(manager: FragmentManager, newFragment: DialogFragment) {
575→            // DialogFragment.show() will take care of adding the fragment
576→            // in a transaction. We also want to remove any currently showing
577→            // dialog, so make our own transaction and take care of that here.
578→            val ft = manager.beginTransaction()
579→            val prev = manager.findFragmentByTag(DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
580→            if (prev != null) {

582→            }
583→            // save transaction to the back stack
584→            ft.addToBackStack(DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
585→            newFragment.show(ft, DIALOG_FRAGMENT_TAG)
586→            manager.executePendingTransactions()
587→        }

        tempFile.writeText("1\n2\n3")
        val previewerIdsFile = PreviewerIdsFile(tempFile.absolutePath)

                activity.isFinishing,
                `is`(true)
            )
        }

        scenario.close()
        tempFile.delete()
    }
}

package com.ichi2.anki.previewer

import androidx.core.os.bundleOf
import androidx.test.core.app.ActivityScenario
import androidx.test.ext.junit.runners.AndroidJUnit4
import com.ichi2.anki.RobolectricTest
import com.ichi2.anki.browser.PreviewerIdsFile
import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
import org.hamcrest.Matchers.`is`
import org.junit.Test
import org.junit.runner.RunWith
import java.io.File

@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4::class)
class PreviewerActivityTest : RobolectricTest() {

    @Test
    fun `back button finishes activity`() {
        // Create a PreviewerIdsFile with test card IDs
        val tempDir = File(targetContext.cacheDir, "test")
        tempDir.mkdirs()
        val previewerIdsFile = PreviewerIdsFile(tempDir, listOf(1L, 2L, 3L))

            PreviewerFragment.CARD_IDS_FILE_ARG to previewerIdsFile
        )

        // Launch the activity

            PreviewerActivity.getIntent(
                targetContext,
                PreviewerFragment::class,
                arguments
            )
        )

        scenario.onActivity { activity ->
            // Simulate back button press
            activity.onBackPressedDispatcher.onBackPressed()
            
            // Verify the activity is finishing
            assertThat(
                "Activity should be finishing after back button press",
                activity.isFinishing,
                `is`(true)
            )
        }

        scenario.close()
        previewerIdsFile.delete()
        tempDir.delete()
    }
}"}
